title: Exercise Stress Testing & Ambulatory Monitoring Quiz

questions.text: What is the most frequent indication for an exercise stress test?

answers.text: Establishing the diagnosis of Coronary Artery Disease (CAD)

questions.text: Which of the following is an ABSOLUTE contraindication to exercise stress testing?
What is the optimal duration for continuous progressive exercise during a diagnostic stress test?
The standard Bruce protocol uses what stage length?
The Naughton and Weber protocols use 1 to 2 minute stages with 1 MET increments. They are most appropriate for:

answers.text: Healthy adults seeking a fitness baseline
Patients with limited exercise tolerance, such as compensated CHF

questions.text: Functional capacity can be overestimated by as much as 20% during a treadmill test if:

answers.text: The patient runs instead of walks
The patient grasps the handrails for support
The patient does not warm up

questions.text: ST segment depression at rest of how much makes the exercise ECG less specific and indicates myocardial imaging should be considered?
Which statement about exercise-induced ST-segment depression is TRUE?

answers.text: It localizes the site of ischemia
It identifies which coronary artery is involved
It does NOT localize the site of ischemia or identify the involved artery

questions.text: Exercise-induced ST-segment elevation of 1 mm or more occurs MOST frequently in patients with:
What is the normal peak systolic blood pressure response to maximal exercise?
Which of the following is an ABNORMAL blood pressure response to exercise?

answers.text: Systolic BP rising progressively from 130 to 180 mmHg
Failure to increase systolic BP beyond 120 mmHg

questions.text: A stress test is considered non-diagnostic if the patient is unable to reach approximately what percentage of age-predicted maximum heart rate?
In an upright cool-down position, what 1-minute heart rate recovery value is considered abnormal?
Which of the following is a reason to TERMINATE an exercise stress test?

answers.text: Drop in systolic BP > 10 mmHg from baseline
Heart rate reaching 70% of predicted maximum
Patient asking to slow down briefly

questions.text: Sustained ventricular tachycardia during a stress test is:

answers.text: A reason to terminate the test

questions.text: What emergency equipment must always be readily available during exercise stress testing?
What is the typical recording duration of a Holter monitor?
An event monitor is typically worn for up to:
Which patient instruction is correct for both Holter and event monitor wear?

answers.text: Take a daily shower while wearing the monitor
Pull on the electrode leads if they feel loose
Go about normal daily activities, but do NOT get the monitor wet (sweat is the only exception)
Open and inspect the recorder daily

questions.text: A clear advantage of an event monitor over a 24–72 hour Holter for documenting INFREQUENT symptoms is that the event monitor:

answers.text: Records continuously for many more days, capturing rare events
Eliminates the need for skin prep
